sourceMovie.title: Idle Hands
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Idle Hands (1999) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Rodman Flender's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Aquamarine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Idle Hands clicked for you, Aquamarine (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dracula
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Idle Hands clicked for you, Dracula (1979) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Idle Hands?
faq.answer: Try Aquamarine, Dracula, Dave Made a Maze—each shares DNA with Idle Hands while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Idle Hands?
faq.answer: Idle Hands (1999) is best described as Comedy, Horror, Fantasy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Idle Hands worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
